meta.name: CET-4 核心 2500 词
meta.source: 教育部考试中心 CET-4 词汇大纲

definitions.definition: to leave a place, thing, or person permanently
to stop doing something before it is finished
complete freedom of behaviour

examples.en: He abandoned his wife and children.
examples.zh: 他抛弃了妻子和孩子。

examples.en: They had to abandon the car on the side of the road.
examples.zh: 他们不得不把车弃在路边。

definitions.definition: existing in thought or as an idea but not having a physical existence
relating to art that does not represent real things
a short written summary of a longer text

examples.en: The idea is too abstract for young children.
examples.zh: 这个概念对幼儿来说太抽象了。

examples.en: Please write an abstract of the article.
examples.zh: 请写一篇这篇文章的摘要。

definitions.definition: relating to education and scholarship
not having any practical purpose
a teacher or researcher at a university

examples.en: She had a brilliant academic career.
examples.zh: 她有辉煌的学术生涯。

examples.en: The debate is purely academic at this point.
examples.zh: 这场辩论在现阶段纯粹是学术性的。

definitions.definition: the right or opportunity to use or benefit from something
a means of entering or approaching a place
to obtain or retrieve information from a computer system

examples.en: Students have access to the library 24 hours a day.
examples.zh: 学生可以24小时使用图书馆。

examples.en: You can access the data from any computer.
examples.zh: 你可以从任何电脑访问数据。

definitions.definition: to provide lodging or sufficient space for
to fit in with the wishes or needs of someone

examples.en: The hotel can accommodate up to 500 guests.
examples.zh: 这家酒店最多可以容纳500位客人。

examples.en: We will try our best to accommodate your special needs.
examples.zh: 我们会尽量满足你的特殊需求。

definitions.definition: to buy or obtain an asset or object for oneself
to learn or develop a skill, habit, or quality

examples.en: She acquired a taste for expensive wine.
examples.zh: 她养成了对高档葡萄酒的爱好。

examples.en: The company acquired a smaller rival.
examples.zh: 公司收购了一家较小的竞争对手。

definitions.definition: to make suitable for a new use or purpose
to become adjusted to new conditions

examples.en: It took him a while to adapt to the new environment.
examples.zh: 他花了一段时间才适应新环境。

examples.en: The film was adapted from a best-selling novel.
examples.zh: 这部电影改编自一本畅销小说。

definitions.definition: satisfactory or acceptable in quality or quantity
barely sufficient or suitable

examples.en: We have adequate supplies for the trip.
examples.zh: 我们有足够的旅行补给。

examples.en: His performance was barely adequate.
examples.zh: 他的表现勉强合格。

definitions.definition: to have an influence on or make a difference to
to make someone feel a strong emotion

examples.en: The weather can affect your mood.
examples.zh: 天气会影响你的心情。

examples.en: She was deeply affected by the news.
examples.zh: 她被这个消息深深打动了。

definitions.definition: to have enough money to pay for
to be able to do something without adverse consequences

examples.en: I can't afford to buy a new car.
examples.zh: 我买不起新车。

examples.en: We can't afford to waste any more time.
examples.zh: 我们再也浪费不起时间了。

definitions.definition: ready or likely to attack or confront
pursuing one's aims and interests forcefully

entries.translation: 好斗的;有进取心的;侵略的

examples.en: He became aggressive when he was drunk.
examples.zh: 他喝醉后变得好斗。

examples.en: The company adopted an aggressive marketing strategy.
examples.zh: 公司采取了激进的营销策略。

definitions.definition: one of two or more available possibilities
available as another possibility

entries.translation: 替代品;可供选择的;非传统的

examples.en: We have no alternative but to cancel the trip.
examples.zh: 我们别无选择只能取消旅行。

examples.en: Public transport is a good alternative to driving.
examples.zh: 公共交通是开车的好替代方式。

definitions.definition: a strong desire to do or achieve something
desire and determination to achieve success

examples.en: His ambition is to become a doctor.
examples.zh: 他的抱负是成为一名医生。

examples.en: She has plenty of ambition but lacks experience.
examples.zh: 她很有抱负但缺乏经验。

definitions.definition: to examine methodically and in detail
to discover or reveal the nature of something by analysis

examples.en: We need to analyze the data carefully.
examples.zh: 我们需要仔细分析数据。

examples.en: The report analyzes the causes of poverty.
examples.zh: 这份报告分析了贫困的原因。

definitions.definition: occurring once every year
a book or magazine published once a year

examples.en: The company's annual profit was $2 million.
examples.zh: 公司年利润为200万美元。

examples.en: The annual meeting will be held in March.
examples.zh: 年会将在三月举行。

definitions.definition: to regard as probable or expect
to act in advance of

examples.en: We anticipate that sales will rise next year.
examples.zh: 我们预计明年销售额将上升。

examples.en: Try to anticipate problems before they occur.
examples.zh: 尽量在问题出现之前预防。

definitions.definition: a feeling of worry, nervousness, or unease
a strong desire or concern to do something

examples.en: She felt a lot of anxiety before the exam.
examples.zh: 考试前她感到非常焦虑。

examples.en: His anxiety to please everyone made him exhausted.
examples.zh: 他渴望取悦每个人使他筋疲力尽。

definitions.definition: clearly visible or understood
seeming real or true, but not necessarily so

examples.en: It was apparent that she was not happy.
examples.zh: 很明显她不开心。

examples.en: His apparent confidence hid deep insecurities.
examples.zh: 他表面上的自信掩盖了深层的不安全感。

definitions.definition: to make a serious or urgent request
the quality of being attractive or interesting
a formal request to a court for a decision to be changed

examples.en: The charity appealed for donations.
examples.zh: 慈善机构呼吁捐款。

examples.en: The idea doesn't appeal to me.
examples.zh: 这个想法对我没有吸引力。

definitions.definition: a natural desire to satisfy a bodily need for food
a strong desire or liking for something

examples.en: Exercise gives you a good appetite.
examples.zh: 运动能让你胃口好。

examples.en: He has an appetite for adventure.
examples.zh: 他有冒险的欲望。

definitions.definition: to make a formal application or request
to put into operation or use
to be relevant or appropriate

examples.en: She applied for a job at the bank.
examples.zh: 她申请了银行的工作。

examples.en: The same rules apply to everyone.
examples.zh: 同样的规则适用于所有人。

definitions.definition: to come near to in distance or time
a way of dealing with something
the act of coming near

examples.en: Winter is approaching.
examples.zh: 冬天快到了。

examples.en: We need a new approach to the problem.
examples.zh: 我们需要一种新的解决问题的方法。

definitions.definition: suitable or proper in the circumstances
to take something for one's own use

examples.en: Jeans are not appropriate for a formal dinner.
examples.zh: 牛仔裤不适合正式晚宴。

examples.en: The government appropriated funds for education.
examples.zh: 政府拨款用于教育。

definitions.definition: to officially agree to or accept as satisfactory
to believe that someone or something is good or acceptable

examples.en: The committee approved the new plan.
examples.zh: 委员会批准了新计划。

examples.en: Her parents don't approve of her boyfriend.
examples.zh: 她的父母不赞成她的男朋友。

definitions.definition: used to show that something is almost, but not completely, accurate

examples.en: The journey takes approximately two hours.
examples.zh: 旅程大约需要两个小时。

examples.en: Approximately 500 people attended the event.
examples.zh: 大约500人参加了活动。

definitions.definition: a particular part or feature of something
the appearance or look of something

examples.en: We need to consider all aspects of the problem.
examples.zh: 我们需要考虑问题的所有方面。

examples.en: Climate change affects every aspect of our lives.
examples.zh: 气候变化影响我们生活的各个层面。

definitions.definition: to evaluate or estimate the nature, quality, or ability
to determine the amount of a tax or fine

examples.en: The teacher will assess your progress at the end of the term.
examples.zh: 老师将在期末评估你的进步。

examples.en: Damage was assessed at $1 million.
examples.zh: 损失评估为100万美元。

definitions.definition: to allocate a task or duty to someone
to transfer or ascribe rights or qualities

examples.en: The teacher assigned homework to the students.
examples.zh: 老师给学生布置了作业。

examples.en: They assigned him to the project.
examples.zh: 他们把他分配到项目组。

definitions.definition: to help someone by doing a task or providing information
an act of helping

examples.en: The nurse assisted the doctor during the surgery.
examples.zh: 护士在手术中协助医生。

examples.en: Technology can assist in learning.
examples.zh: 技术可以辅助学习。

definitions.definition: to connect someone or something in one's mind
to spend time together
a partner or colleague in business

examples.en: Most people associate this brand with quality.
examples.zh: 大多数人把这个品牌和质量联系在一起。

examples.en: She is a business associate of mine.
examples.zh: 她是我的商业伙伴。

definitions.definition: to suppose to be the case, without proof
to take or begin to have power or responsibility

examples.en: I assume you already know about the meeting.
examples.zh: 我猜你已经知道开会的事了。

examples.en: She assumed responsibility for the project.
examples.zh: 她承担了项目的责任。

definitions.definition: to make an effort to achieve or complete
an act of trying to do something

examples.en: He attempted to escape from prison.
examples.zh: 他企图越狱。

examples.en: It was her first attempt at climbing a mountain.
examples.zh: 那是她第一次尝试登山。

definitions.definition: to cause to come to a place or participate by offering something interesting
to evoke interest or liking in someone

examples.en: The museum attracts millions of visitors each year.
examples.zh: 博物馆每年吸引数百万游客。

examples.en: Opposite poles attract each other.
examples.zh: 异性磁极互相吸引。

definitions.definition: the power or right to give orders and enforce obedience
a person or organization having political or administrative power
an expert on a subject

examples.en: The local authorities are investigating the incident.
examples.zh: 当地当局正在调查此事件。

examples.en: She is an authority on ancient Chinese history.
examples.zh: 她是古代中国历史的权威。

definitions.definition: able to be used or obtained
not busy; free to do something

entries.translation: 可获得的;可用的;有空的

examples.en: Tickets are available online.
examples.zh: 门票可以在网上购买。

examples.en: Are you available for a meeting tomorrow?
examples.zh: 你明天有空开会吗？

definitions.definition: having knowledge or perception of a situation or fact
having interest in and concern about a particular subject

entries.translation: 意识到的;知道的;有意识的

examples.en: Are you aware of the risks?
examples.zh: 你意识到这些风险了吗？

examples.en: She is very aware of current events.
examples.zh: 她非常关注时事。

definitions.definition: a fence or obstacle that prevents movement or access
a circumstance or obstacle that keeps people apart or prevents progress

examples.en: Language can be a barrier to communication.
examples.zh: 语言可能成为沟通的障碍。

examples.en: The police put up barriers to control the crowd.
examples.zh: 警方设置了路障来控制人群。

definitions.definition: an advantage or profit gained from something
to receive an advantage; to be helped by

examples.en: Exercise has many health benefits.
examples.zh: 运动有很多健康益处。

examples.en: Students will benefit from the new program.
examples.zh: 学生将从新项目中受益。

definitions.definition: to assign responsibility for a fault or wrong
responsibility for a fault or wrong

examples.en: Don't blame me for your mistakes.
examples.zh: 别把你的错误怪到我头上。

examples.en: She took the blame for the accident.
examples.zh: 她承担了事故的责任。

definitions.definition: to help or encourage to increase or improve
an act of helping or encouraging an increase or improvement

examples.en: The good news boosted our morale.
examples.zh: 好消息鼓舞了我们的士气。

examples.en: The tax cut gave a boost to the economy.
examples.zh: 减税提振了经济。
